children.name: Tyto
children.description: Tyto — a genus of barn owls & bay owls in the family Tytonidae.

children.name: Tyto alba
children.description: The western barn owl is a species of barn owl native to Europe, southwestern Asia, and Africa. It was formerly considered a subspecies group together with barn owls native to other parts of the world, but this classification was found to be paraphyletic with respect to some other members of the genus.

children.name: Tyto almae
children.description: The Seram masked owl is an owl species in the family Tytonidae endemic to Seram Island in Indonesia, first described in 2013. It may be a subspecies of the Moluccan masked owl.

children.name: Tyto aurantia
children.description: The golden masked owl is a barn owl endemic to the island of New Britain, Papua New Guinea. It is also known as New Britain barn owl, New Britain masked owl, Bismarck owl and Bismarck masked owl.

children.name: Tyto balearica
children.description: Tyto balearica is an extinct species of Tyto that lived during the Neogene and Quaternary.

children.name: Tyto capensis
children.description: The African grass owl or simply grass owl is a species of owl in the barn owl family, Tytonidae.

children.name: Tyto cavatica
children.description: The Puerto Rican barn owl is an extinct species of barn owl that inhabited the island of Puerto Rico in the Caribbean. It is sometimes considered to be a subspecies of the ashy-faced owl.

children.name: Tyto cravesae
children.description: Tyto cravesae is an extinct species of Tyto that inhabited Cuba during the Quaternary period.

children.name: Tyto deroepstorffi
children.description: The Andaman masked owl is a barn owl endemic to the southern Andaman Islands archipelago of India, in the Bay of Bengal, Indian Ocean. Regarded by some authors as a subspecies of the common barn owl, it is recognized by others as a species in its own right.

children.name: Tyto furcata
children.description: The American barn owl is usually considered a subspecies group and together with the western barn owl group, the eastern barn owl group, and sometimes the Andaman masked owl, make up the barn owl, cosmopolitan in range. The barn owl is recognized by most taxonomic authorities. A few separate them into distinct species, as is done here.

children.name: Tyto gigantea
children.description: Tyto gigantea — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto glaucops
children.description: The ashy-faced owl is a species of bird in the barn-owl family Tytonidae. It is found on the Caribbean island of Hispaniola, on Dominica, and on several other islands of the Lesser Antilles.

children.name: Tyto inexspectata
children.description: The Minahasa masked owl, also known as the Minahasa barn owl, Sulawesi owl or Sulawesi golden owl, is a barn owl endemic to the island of Sulawesi, Indonesia. The name is derived from the Minahassa Peninsula, where it was first described as a breeding bird; however, it is also known to live in north-central Sulawesi.

children.name: Tyto javanica
children.description: Tyto javanica — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto letocarti
children.description: Tyto letocarti — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto longimembris
children.description: The eastern grass owl, also known as Chinese grass owl or Australasian grass owl, is a species of owl in the family Tytonidae. They feed predominantly on small rodents.

children.name: Tyto manusi
children.description: The Manus masked owl is a barn owl endemic to Manus Island in the Admiralty Islands. Some authors consider it a subspecies of Australian masked owl (Tyto novaehollandiae).

children.name: Tyto mourerchauvireae
children.description: Tyto mourerchauvireae — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto multipunctata
children.description: The lesser sooty owl is a medium-sized masked owl endemic to the wet tropics region of Australia. Once considered a subspecies of the greater sooty owl, it is distinguished by its dark plumage, heavier spotting, and higher-pitched call. The lesser sooty owl has a limited range within northeastern Australia and primarily lives in dark, sheltered areas of the rainforest.

children.name: Tyto nigrobrunnea
children.description: The Taliabu masked owl, also known as the Taliabu owl or the Sula Islands barn owl, is an owl in the barn owl family, Tytonidae. This is one of the two groups of owls, the other being the typical owls, family Strigidae. It is endemic to Taliabu Island of Indonesia.

children.name: Tyto noeli
children.description: Tyto noeli — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto novaehollandiae
children.description: Tyto novaehollandiae — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto ostologa
children.description: Tyto ostologa — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto pollens
children.description: Tyto pollens — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto pratinicola
children.description: Tyto pratinicola — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto rosenbergii
children.description: The Sulawesi masked owl is a species of owl in the family Tytonidae. It is endemic to the Indonesian islands of Sulawesi, Sangihe and Peleng. It is listed by the IUCN as being of least concern.

children.name: Tyto sororcula
children.description: Tyto sororcula — a species in the genus Tyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Tyto soumagnei
children.description: The red owl is an owl in the barn owl family Tytonidae. It is also known as the Madagascar red owl, Madagascar grass-owl, Soumagne's owl or lesser grass-owl. It is a rare resident of Madagascar that was virtually unknown from its discovery in 1876 to its rediscovery by researchers from the World Wide Fund for Nature in 1993.

children.name: Tyto tenebricosa
children.description: The sooty owl or greater sooty owl is a medium to large owl found in south-eastern Australia, Montane rainforests of New Guinea and have been seen on Flinders Island in the Bass Strait. The lesser sooty owl, is sometimes considered to be conspecific with this species, in which case they are then together referred to as sooty owls. It is substantially smaller and occurs in the wet tropics region of North Queensland, Australia.

children.name: Phodilus
children.description: Phodilus — a genus of barn owls & bay owls in the family Tytonidae.

children.name: Phodilus assimilis
children.description: Phodilus assimilis — a species in the genus Phodilus, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Phodilus badius
children.description: Phodilus badius — a species in the genus Phodilus, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Phodilus prigoginei
children.description: Phodilus prigoginei — a species in the genus Phodilus, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Prosybris
children.description: Prosybris — a genus of barn owls & bay owls in the family Tytonidae.

children.name: Necrobyas medius
children.description: Necrobyas medius — a species in the genus Prosybris, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Prosybris antiqua
children.description: Prosybris antiqua — a species in the genus Prosybris, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Palaeotyto
children.description: Palaeotyto — a genus of barn owls & bay owls in the family Tytonidae.

children.name: Palaeotyto cadurcensis
children.description: Palaeotyto cadurcensis — a species in the genus Palaeotyto, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Palaeoglaux artophoron
children.description: Palaeoglaux artophoron — a species in the genus Palaeoglaux, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.name: Palaeoglaux perrierensis
children.description: Palaeoglaux perrierensis — a species in the genus Palaeoglaux, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.description: Lechusa stirtoni — a species in the genus Lechusa, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.

children.description: Palaeobyas cracrafti — a species in the genus Palaeobyas, family Tytonidae. Also known as barn owls & bay owls.
